subject: Update on your interview - Round 1 passed!
body:
Hi Will,
Congrats on moving on to Round 2 of interviews. We elected for the take home in Round 2 as the team is traveling.

The project: Build a system with three agents that work together around the use case that most excites you. Use whichever model and best practices you would like.
 Beyond getting it working, we want to see two things:
  1. How you'd evaluate it
  2. How you'd improve it 

There's no single right answer. We're more interested in how you think about agent quality than in a perfect implementation.

What to send us: A GitHub repo with your code and a short README explaining your design, your evaluation approach, and what you'd do differently with more time.

Time: As much time as you believe necessary. We expect to evaluate early next week.

Looking forward to seeing what you build. Feel free to text me if you have any questions
Best,

--
Bobby Trickett

subject: GitHub: Nexus-Demo repo created
body: You have successfully created the repository Nexus-Demo.

subject: Inquiry: Staff AI Engineer at Acme Corp
body:
Hi William,

I came across your profile and was impressed by your background. We are currently looking for a Staff AI Engineer to lead our new multi-agent systems team.

We specifically need someone with deep expertise in LangGraph, deterministic routing, and human-in-the-loop systems. Do you have any recent projects or architecture demos that showcase these skills? We'd love to chat if you're open to new opportunities.

Best,
Sarah Jenkins
Acme Corp

subject: Contract gig: Integrating LLMs with Personal Data
body:
Hey William,

A mutual connection mentioned you were building some cool stuff with local agents. We are trying to build an agent that can reason over a user's local markdown files (like an Obsidian vault) without compromising privacy.

We are struggling with how to route requests and keep the LLM from hallucinating files. Have you built anything similar? If so, what was your approach to the architecture?

Let me know if you're available for a consulting call this week.

Cheers,
David

subject: Lab Results: Annual Panel
body:
Hello William,

Your recent blood panel results are back. Everything looks mostly normal, but your Vitamin D levels are a bit low (22 ng/mL). I recommend starting a daily supplement of 2000 IU and getting some more sun exposure.

Please log this in your personal health tracker.

Best,
Dr. Thorne
